ITER Vacuum Vessel — Lateral Rattle Analysis

2026-05-27  ·  5,000-sample Monte Carlo  ·  scipy HiGHS LP  ·  3 DOF model (Δx, Δy, Δθ)  ·  R = 8 m  ·  9 gravity supports

Key numbers: LP bound (nominal assembly) = 3.09 mm  ·  MC P95 = 2.28 mm  ·  MC P99 = 2.56 mm  ·  Rotation range = 0.375 mrad

§1 — Physical Mechanism

The VV sits on 9 equally-spaced four-bar linkages that allow radial motion (thermal growth, assembly). At the top of each four-bar a toroidally-oriented pin limits lateral (toroidal) travel to ±1.5 mm about the assembly position. Any lateral force causes the VV to rattle within this envelope.

Constraint per support i:  δᵢ = −sin(φᵢ)·Δx + cos(φᵢ)·Δy + R·Δθ  ≤  1.5 − uᵢ
                           −δᵢ                                        ≤  1.5 + uᵢ

§2 — State Diagrams

Displacements magnified ×500. Blue ring = VV position. Orange spoke = support 0 (orientation marker). Blue shaded polygon = rattle polytope (all reachable VV centre positions). Dots = pin positions.

§3 — Translation Rattle Key Frames

Five key positions along the principal rattle axis. Nominal assembly (u=0), ×500 magnification.

§4 — Rotation Mode Key Frames

Maximum rotation range ±187.5 µrad = ±5.37° at ×500. All 9 brackets slide synchronously.

§5 — Worst-Case Assembly: Translation Key Frames

§6 — Why Rattle Exceeds 3 mm

Single bracket travel is ±1.5 mm; nominal rattle is 3.09 mm (full back-to-front range). Explanation:

Supportφ (°)Δx coefficientNotes
0 (top)90−1.00LP uses rotation to neutralise this constraint
4−70+0.94Binding at +X optimum: limits to 1.5/0.94 = 1.60 mm
5−110+0.94Binding at +X optimum

LP simultaneously sets Δθ = +5.83 µrad, which exactly cancels the top-support constraint, allowing Δx = 1.547 mm (half-rattle). Full range = 2 × 1.547 = 3.09 mm.

§7 — Monte Carlo Results (5,000 samples)

Statisticmm
LP bound (nominal)3.09
MC P992.556
MC P952.282
MC P902.119
MC P50 (median)1.514

§8 — Effect of Gap Measurements

Measuring 4 adjacent supports reduces P95 from 2.28 mm to ≈1.95 mm. Measuring all 9 gives an exact deterministic answer (LP only, no MC).

§9 — Recommendations

#ActionStatus
1Use 3.09 mm as bounding rattle for nominal designCONFIRMED
2Use P95 = 2.28 mm as statistical bound for random assembliesCONFIRMED
3Measure ≥4 bracket gaps before final closureRECOMMENDED
4Measure all 9 for exact deterministic LP answerIDEAL
5Include rotation (0.375 mrad) in seismic/dynamic loads analysisRECOMMENDED
6Verify port connections / umbilicals accommodate 3.09 mm + rotationACTION REQUIRED

Source: Simon-McIntosh/vv  ·  vv_rattle_mc.py (LP solver) + vv_viz.py (visualisation).
